pawnbroker: Cash on the counter, kid. We're not running a tab.
That one's been sitting in the case since '64. It works.
Don't ask me about the medal. Wear it or don't.
You remind me of someone.
Not that one. Trust me.
Pay up front. The dead don't carry change.
Your grandfather paid full price too.
Lord watches drunks and fools. Bring backup anyway.
If it shines, it's a fake. If it tarnishes, it's mine.
The price is the price. Walk it off.
Last man who haggled with me is in the East River.
You getting hungry yet? There's a place on Mulberry.
Don't shoot in the bodega. Mr. Halpern still owes me.
Eat before you go. You can't aim on an empty stomach.
Take the medal. Just take it.
Some nights the door is wax-sealed. Don't break it. Try again next week.
Bring it back if it jams. Don't bring it back if it doesn't.
I had a brother who carried that. He came home most of the time.
Sanitation called twice this morning. Twice.
The kid before you was a quiet one too. Quieter than you.
There's coffee. There's always coffee.
rumorMill: They say the Bowery missed three pickups in a row and the city sent a man in instead of a truck.
They say a kid on Avenue C went down a storm drain on a dare in August and came back up wrong.
They say the bishop at Old St. Patrick's has been drawing a different cathedral floor plan every night and won't stop.
They say the cooling tower at Indian Point glowed green for eleven minutes one night last spring and Con Ed denies it.
They say a sanitation foreman in Red Hook keeps a list of addresses he won't pick up from and the list gets longer every week.
They say the man who used to run the pet shop on Bleecker has not been seen since the blackout but the pet shop is still open and someone is still feeding the snakes.
They say there is a freight elevator in the basement of the Woolworth Building that goes one floor lower than the basement.
They say the priest at St. Anthony's on Sullivan keeps a shotgun behind the lectern but he won't say who he learned from.
They say a longshoreman went down to the East River on All Souls' last year and came back without his shadow.
They say if you go to the shop at 142 Mott and the door is wax-sealed, don't break it. Walk away. Try again next week.
